mission: Audit local MCP exposure on this Mac workstation following the April 2026 MCP stdio-transport vulnerability (OX Security disclosure, CVE-2026-30615 family). Produce a read-only risk report. Do not modify, rotate, delete, install, commit, or push anything.
operator: Dr Mubashir (Malik Mubashir Hassan). Working directory: /Users/mmh/Documents/Claude/Projects/Tenu.World

threat_model.vulnerability: Unsafe defaults in MCP STDIO transport allow RCE on host when the MCP server processes untrusted input.
threat_model.tenu_production_exposure: Claimed nil. Tenu calls the Anthropic REST API from server-side Next.js route handlers. No MCP runtime in prod. This audit must verify that claim.
threat_model.secondary_blast_radius: If a vulnerable MCP server runs on this Mac and its config or spawn-cwd holds Tenu secrets in plaintext, workstation RCE leaks those secrets.

tasks.title: Confirm Tenu has no MCP dependency
actions: From the Tenu repo root, run: grep -nE '@modelcontextprotocol|langchain|litellm|langflow|flowise|lettaai|langbot' package.json package-lock.json
Record: Tenu production MCP exposure = GREEN if no match. RED with file and line number if any match.

tasks.title: Inventory MCP client configs on this Mac
actions: For each file found: parse JSON, list server names, transport (stdio|sse|http), command, args. Note whether the 'env' block is present and non-empty. Never print env values.

tasks.title: Scan MCP configs for plaintext secrets
actions: For each file from T2, run grep -nE against the patterns listed in 'patterns' below.
For each hit, report: file, line number, pattern label, and first 4 characters followed by ****.
Never print full or middle of any matched value.
Flag RED if any hit. GREEN if none.

tasks.title: Cross-reference installed MCP servers against the known-vulnerable list
actions: For each MCP server identified in T2, extract the npm package or python module name from the 'command' plus 'args' fields.
Run: pip show litellm langchain langflow flowise letta langbot 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^(Name|Version):'
Flag YELLOW for any match pending vendor patch. Record version strings verbatim.

tasks.title: Map MCP server spawn paths to nearby .env files
actions: For each MCP server in T2 with a spawn-cwd or script path, check whether the parent directory contains .env, .env.local, or .env.production.
Do not print values. Report only counts per secret name.
Flag RED if any count greater than zero for a Tenu secret sharing a directory with a stdio-transport MCP server.

deliverable.format: Markdown. Title block, TL;DR (GREEN or YELLOW or RED with one-line reason), sections T1..T5, remediation checklist ordered by blast radius, and a final block of proposed TASKS.md lines Dr Mubashir can paste in.
rules: Never include full secret values. First 4 characters plus **** maximum.
Never include absolute paths inside ~/.ssh/, ~/.aws/credentials, or browser keychain files. Those are out of scope.
If the file already exists from a prior run, append a new dated section rather than overwriting.

constraints: Read-only. Zero writes outside the deliverable path.
No npm install, pip install, brew, cap sync, cap add, or any mutation command.
No git add, no git commit, no git push.
No API calls to Anthropic, Stripe, Supabase, Cloudflare, Brevo, or GitHub.
If a command would take more than 30 seconds (for example a find on ~ that is slow), skip it and note the skip in the report.
stop_conditions: Stop and print a short warning if any finding suggests active compromise (unexpected .bash_history entries writing to a Cloudflare endpoint, unknown launchd items, MCP servers added in the last 72 hours).
Stop and ask Dr Mubashir before proceeding if T3 finds a real secret pattern AND T4 flags a vulnerable MCP server in the same config.
success_criteria: Deliverable file written with all five T sections completed or explicitly marked SKIPPED with reason.
TL;DR reflects the worst single flag across T1..T5.
No mutation on disk outside the deliverable file.
